content:
# About the Author

## Sunny R Gupta

**Sunny R Gupta** is an engineering leader, writer, and builder based in Bengaluru, India.

He currently leads engineering at **JioHotstar**, India's largest streaming platform — a product used by hundreds of millions of people. Before that, he spent years at **Atlassian**, the company behind Jira, Confluence, and Trello, where he worked on developer tooling at global scale.

He's also a founder: **TeamShiksha** started as a passion project around education, and **LithSocial** is his current build-in-public venture. He tends to be making something at all times.

---

### The Unlikely Engineer

Sunny's path into software wasn't the one on the brochure. He didn't crack JEE. He didn't get a computer science degree. By his own telling, he was a 60–80% student.

He graduated with the highest campus placement CTC his college had ever recorded.

That gap — between credentials and capability, between the expected path and the actual one — is something he thinks about a lot. It shapes how he writes, how he leads teams, and how he teaches. He has never believed that pedigree predicts potential.

---

### The "for Bunnies" Series

*Building Apps with AI for Bunnies* is part of a series Sunny created to make technical concepts accessible to anyone curious enough to try.

The series started with **"System Design for Bunnies"** (January 2026), which explained distributed systems through the lens of a broken messenger — using narrative, analogy, and the deliberate avoidance of jargon. It resonated. Widely.

The series name isn't accidental. Bunnies are curious, quick, and underestimated. Sunny writes for people who feel like they don't quite belong in the room — and who are going to prove that feeling wrong.

---

### Writing & Thinking

Sunny writes regularly on LinkedIn and X, where he has built an audience of over 65,000 people across both platforms. His pieces tend to sit at the intersection of technology and the human experience of working in it.

Recent work includes *"Managers Have Always Been Vibe Coders. We Just Didn't Have the Words for It."* — a piece arguing that product leadership has always been a form of intent-driven programming, long before anyone called it that.

He's the kind of writer who finds the unexpected angle and pulls on it until something interesting comes out.

---

### Outside the Code

When not building or writing, Sunny does photography and videography for a non-profit school that educates under-privileged children in Bengaluru — work he's been doing through Atlassian Foundation since 2020.

He also ran the Web Services arm of a Rotaract chapter earlier in his career, which is where he learned that you can care deeply about technology *and* about the people it's supposed to serve.

---

**Connect with Sunny:**

- LinkedIn: [linkedin.com/in/sunnykgupta](https://linkedin.com/in/sunnykgupta)
- X / Twitter: [@sunnykgupta](https://x.com/sunnykgupta)
